messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).

The Meditations (沉思錄) is attributed to Marcus Aurelius, the Roman emperor and Stoic philosopher who lived from 121 to 180 CE. The confidence in this attribution is strong — it is regarded as a personal philosophical journal he wrote for his own self-improvement, not originally intended for publication. It should not be attributed to Plato, Socrates, or Epictetus, despite the Stoic themes that connect it to the broader Greco-Roman philosophical tradition. Epictetus in particular was an important Stoic influence on Marcus Aurelius, but the Meditations is Marcus Aurelius' own work.
